pattern: DP over modular remainder classes: for each target remainder r, track the longest subsequence where consecutive sums are congruent to r mod k.
hints: Fix a target remainder r in [0, k). A valid subsequence with all consecutive sums equal to r mod k means each element's value mod k determines what the next element's mod must be.

Define dp[r][v] as the length of the longest valid subsequence ending with a number whose value mod k equals v and whose consecutive-sum remainder is r. Transitioning from a prior element with mod u to current mod v requires (u + v) % k == r, so u = (r - v + k) % k.
Iterate over elements; for each element compute its mod v, then for each r update dp[r][v] = dp[r][(r-v+k)%k] + 1. The answer is the maximum over all dp[r][v].

edge_inputs.description: all elements same — consecutive sum is 2*val mod k
k=1 — all consecutive sums are 0 mod 1 — full length
k=2 — reduces to parity alternation case
k larger than all values — only single-element subsequences satisfy some r
repeating pattern with period 2 under k=3

stress.description: random arrays of length up to 100 with random k up to 50
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10**7) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 50)
    return [nums, k]
